info.title: Brainiac
info.description: GitOps for organizational AI knowledge — capture, govern, and serve an organization's memory. Every read is RLS-scoped to the caller: an agent can never retrieve what its operator cannot.

get.description: Liveness probe; unauthenticated.

get.summary:
The document itself. Open (like `/health`) — a client needs the contract
before it has a token, and the spec describes shapes, never data.
get.description: This OpenAPI document, derived from the handlers.
Governance health counters under the caller's RLS view: memories by status, review backlog, graph size, ingest queue depth.

The leadership Knowledge Health report: a tracked composite score over four pillars (consistency, currency, liquidity, governance), the org-level signals behind it, and a ranked attention list. RLS-scoped — a leader sees their org's view.

post.summary:
Record a Knowledge Health snapshot for the caller's org — the tick that builds
the trend line. An org runs this on a schedule (their cron or ours); each call
captures the current score + pillars + flagship signals into history. Needs a
`write` principal, since it mutates. Returns the snapshot it took.
post.description: Record a Knowledge Health snapshot into the org's trend history (call weekly). Returns the captured score + grade.

get.description: The dashboard payload: status totals, weekly captured/promoted flow, kind×team volumes, top canonical themes, review latency, contradiction mix, queue depth.

Practice divergences the org should standardize — cross-team clusters an LLM sweep judged to be the SAME practice solved DIFFERENTLY, each with a recommended standard. RLS-scoped. Populated by the `scan-divergence` sweep.

get.summary:
Reverse-chronological feed of governance actions: promotion reviews
(human and policy), contradiction resolutions, and dispute resolutions.
Reuses the reviewer / resolved-by columns the tables already carry; rows
resolve under the caller's RLS transaction so members see their org slice
only.
get.description: Reverse-chronological feed of governance actions: promotion reviews (human and policy), contradiction resolutions, and dispute resolutions.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Narrow to one action kind: promotion_review | contradiction_resolution | feedback_resolution

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
Optional lexical query: filter pages by title, slug, or body (F-5 — the
doc search the MCP surface already had, now over REST). Omit to list all.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description:
Exact page kind: `entity_page` | `topic_page` | `runbook` | `onboarding`
| `digest` | `standards_page`. Unknown kinds are a 400, not an empty
list — a typo must not read as "no runbooks exist".

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description:
Canonical entity name (case-insensitive): pages citing a memory anchored
to that entity. The same vocabulary the OKF bundle publishes as `tags`.

parameters.name: stale
parameters.description:
`true` = only stale pages (an underlying memory moved), `false` = only
current ones. Omit for both.

parameters.name: space
parameters.description:
The wiki folder (first slug segment, e.g. `payments`). What the tree
browses one space at a time.

parameters.name: needs_review
parameters.description: `true` = only pages whose current revision awaits a human (review tab).

parameters.name: facets
parameters.description:
Compute the cross-filtered facet menu (space directory + tab counts).
Off by default so a headless page-walk pays only for the rows it reads.

post.summary:
Publish a pending revision. The same gate as promotions: an agent composed
it; a NAMED HUMAN publishes it. Maintainer of the owning team only — and for
an org-wide page with no owning team, any maintainer in the org, since no
single team owns the org's shared view.
Edit a section of a page.
post.description:
This endpoint is where the document layer's central asymmetry becomes a
product experience rather than an architecture diagram (KB-PLAN D1):

- A **pinned** section is human-owned prose. It saves. Done.
- A **composed** section is a *projection of memories*. Saving the edited
  text into the page would fork the truth: the page would say one thing, the
  memory layer another, and the next recompose would silently revert the
  human — the single most infuriating behaviour a wiki can have. So the edit
  is sent through the EXTRACTION pipeline instead. It becomes candidate
  memories, passes the same review gate as everything else, and the section
  regenerates once they land.

The editor is told exactly that: "your change was captured as proposed
knowledge". Not "saved" — because it wasn't, and a tool that says "saved"
when it means "queued for someone else's approval" has lied to the person
most likely to notice.

get.description: The entity graph: canonical hubs, team-scoped surface entities, and evidence edges (bounded at 2000/2000/5000 rows).

get.summary:
L2/L3: one canonical entity's neighborhood — surface forms per team,
1-hop evidence edges (content RLS-scoped), neighbor canonicals reachable
through those edges, and the anchored memories the caller may read.
get.description: Cortex map L2/L3: one canonical entity's neighborhood — surface forms per team, 1-hop evidence edges, neighbor canonicals, and readable anchored memories.

404.description: Canonical entity not found

get.summary:
L0/L1: team lobes with volumes, top canonical hubs with team spread, and
team-pair binding strength (shared canonicals). Bounded by construction.
get.description: Cortex map L0/L1: team lobes with volumes, the top 60 canonical hubs with their team spread, and team-pair binding strength.

post.description: The L6 bridge: turn a detected practice divergence into a proposed standard candidate carrying the divergence as provenance. Idempotent. Takes lib:publish.

get.description: The org's skill catalog. Every skill is listed; only versions a named human published can be downloaded.

post.description: Propose a skill as a DRAFT (lib:propose). The counterpart to standards/propose for procedures. The outcome is only ever a draft — never served to agents until a named human publishes a version. Deduplicated by name (a duplicate collapses onto the existing skill) and rate-limited per author.

get.description: The current PUBLISHED bundle of a skill. A draft nobody signed is not served — the response is 404, exactly as if no version existed. Downloading records a fetch event, counted by team.
The org's coding standards, adopted rules by default — the set an agent should follow for a stack. RLS-scoped.

parameters.name: lifecycle
parameters.description:
`adopted` (default — what an agent should follow), `proposed`,
`deprecated`, or `all`.

post.description: Propose a standard candidate (lib:propose). The outcome is only ever a PROPOSED candidate — the gate stays human. Deduplicated against the whole corpus (a duplicate collapses onto the existing standard, whatever its lifecycle) and rate-limited per author.

404.description: cited evidence memory not found

get.description: One rule with the provenance behind it — the memories, incidents, and divergences that motivated it, or the mark of the human who decreed it.

post.description: Adopt a proposed standard — the named-human gate. Takes lib:publish. Refused (409) when the rule has neither provenance nor a decree.
Retire an adopted standard, in the open. Takes lib:publish.
Reject a proposed candidate — kept, not deleted: the mining sweep dedups against rejections for the dedup window, so a maintainer who said no is not asked again next week. Takes lib:publish.
Report a usage signal. Attributed to the caller's team — never to a person; the events table has no user column to fill.

get.description: Browse the memory archive, optionally as-of an instant (time travel over the validity windows). Returns the filtered total alongside the page.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Filter by project id, or `none` for org-shared rows

parameters.name: as_of
parameters.description: RFC3339 instant: return rows VALID then, including since-deprecated ones

post.description: Ingest raw content as a source and enqueue the extraction pipeline (async). Pass an `Idempotency-Key` header to make retries safe: the same key (scoped to your org, for the source's lifetime) replays the ORIGINAL receipt instead of minting a duplicate source.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Opaque retry token (≤200 chars). Same key + org ⇒ the original source_id/job_id, no duplicate source.

202.description: Source stored and job enqueued (or the original receipt replayed for a repeated Idempotency-Key)

400.description: Empty content, or an oversized content / Idempotency-Key

post.description: Ingest up to 100 items in one request (org imports). Each item is validated and enqueued independently: the response carries a per-item result in request order, so one bad item ({error, code}) never sinks the others ({source_id, job_id}). This route accepts a larger request body than single add.

202.description: Batch accepted; see per-item results (mix of receipts and errors)

400.description: Empty batch or more than 100 items

get.summary:
The re-verification queue: live candidate/canonical memories whose
validity window closes within the horizon, oldest boundary first.
get.description: The re-verification queue: live candidate/canonical memories whose validity window closes within the horizon, oldest boundary first.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Horizon in days (default 30, clamped 0..3650; 0 = only already-expired)

post.description: Hybrid (vector + lexical + graph) retrieval under the caller's RLS scope.

get.description: The as-of skeleton: {id, valid_from, valid_to, status} for every memory matching the filter (as_of excluded). Tiny by design — it lets a client scrub the archive's time axis instantly without holding the full corpus. Takes the same filters as /v1/memories minus paging.
One memory with its provenance, anchored entities, promotion history, and supersession lineage (signed chain depth: negative = predecessors).

404.description: Memory not found (or invisible under RLS)

post.summary:
POST /v1/memories/{id}/feedback — report how a served memory held up.
Mirrors MCP `memory_feedback` exactly: synonyms are canonicalized, an
invisible memory is a plain 404 (no existence oracle), and the same store
calls run under the caller's RLS.
post.description: Report how a retrieved memory held up: helpful (alias useful), wrong, or outdated (alias stale). Verdicts drive ranking and re-verification.

400.description: Unknown verdict, or an oversized note

404.description: Memory not found (or invisible under RLS — no oracle)

get.summary:
GET /v1/memories/{id}/provenance — trace a memory's evidence chain. Read
scope. Invisible-under-RLS resolves to 404, the same as a nonexistent id
(no existence oracle) — mirrors MCP `memory_provenance`.
get.description: Trace a memory's evidence chain for citation: who/what recorded it, the model, when, the originating source with a short excerpt, and the canonical entities it anchors.

404.description: Memory not found (or invisible under RLS — no oracle)

post.summary:
Re-verification is a governance act: like promotion review, it requires
a maintainer of the owning team (org-wide memories: any org principal
with write scope).
post.description: Re-verify a memory: extend its validity window from now and close any open feedback claims against it. Requires a maintainer of the owning team.

requestBody.description: Optional validity budget; body may be omitted entirely

403.description: Caller is not a maintainer of the owning team

404.description: Memory not found, or superseded (supersessions are final)

post.summary: POST /v1/onboard/poll — the CLI's wait loop; the approving poll mints.
post.description: Poll a pairing request by device_code. While pending returns {status: "pending"}; the first poll after approval claims the request and returns the minted project-scoped key EXACTLY ONCE.

200.description: Current pairing state (token present exactly once, on claim)

404.description: Unknown device code

get.description: Pending pairing requests with their whitelist match in the caller's org (admin). A null project_name means approval will be refused until the remote is registered under a project.

post.summary: POST /v1/onboard/requests/{id}/approve — approve into the whitelist match.
post.description: Approve a pending pairing (admin). The project is DERIVED from the whitelist — the remote must already be registered under a project in the caller's org, or this refuses with 409.

200.description: Approved; the CLI's next poll mints the key

404.description: Request not found, expired, or already decided

409.description: Remote not whitelisted under any project in this org

post.description: Deny a pending pairing request (admin). The polling CLI is told {status: "denied"} and stops.

404.description: Request not found or already decided

get.summary:
GET /v1/onboard/skill — the brainiac-onboard skill, served for the
copyable bootstrap command in the Keys module.
get.description: The brainiac-onboard Claude Code skill (markdown). Open, like /health: a developer fetches this BEFORE they have a token — the skill is how they get one. Canonical source: docs/skills/brainiac-onboard/SKILL.md.

post.description: Open an onboarding pairing request for a git remote. Unauthenticated — this is how a developer with no credentials acquires one. Returns a short user_code (matched by eye in the console) and a long device_code to poll with.

400.description: Remote is not a recognizable git remote URL

429.description: Too many pending pairing requests; try again shortly

get.description: Every configured org-intelligence sweep with its cadence and last-run status. Admin scope.

put.description: Enable/disable a sweep or retune its cadence. Enabling schedules it to run on the next scheduler tick. Admin scope.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Sweep kind: divergence | health_snapshot | raw_ttl | alerts | library

404.description: No such sweep kind

post.description: Trigger a one-shot run now — marks the sweep due so the worker picks it up within ~20s. Works whether or not the sweep is enabled. Admin scope.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Sweep kind: divergence | health_snapshot | raw_ttl | alerts | library

404.description: No such sweep kind

get.summary:
Org directory for the key-mint picker (admin scope — it feeds token
management). Users/teams carry no RLS; org scoping is explicit.
get.description: Org directory for the key-mint picker: every user in the caller's org with their team memberships and roles. Requires admin scope.

get.summary: Recent pipeline runs — the worker's own audit trail, org-scoped by RLS.
get.description: Recent pipeline runs — the worker's own audit trail, newest first, org-scoped by RLS. Paged: `total` reports the full trail, `offset` reaches beyond the first page.

get.summary: GET /v1/projects — the org's projects with their whitelisted repos.
get.description: The org's projects with their whitelisted repos (admin).

post.description: Create a project (admin). Names are unique per org.

409.description: A project with this name already exists

post.summary: POST /v1/projects/{id}/repos — whitelist a remote under a project.
post.description: Whitelist a git remote under a project (admin). The remote is normalized to `host/owner/name`; within an org a remote maps to exactly one project.

400.description: Remote is not a recognizable git remote URL

404.description: Project not found in this org

409.description: This remote is already whitelisted in the org

delete.description: Remove a whitelisted remote (admin). Existing keys stay valid; only future onboarding stops matching.

404.description: Repo not found in this org

post.summary: POST /v1/provision — exchange a verified identity for its project.
post.description: Exchange a VERIFIED federated identity for its single project, optionally minting a device key. Requires the `admin` scope; the caller must have already verified the identity with the provider.

get.description: Dead-lettered jobs, most recent first. Paged: `total` reports the full recovery backlog, `offset` reaches beyond the first page (default limit 50).

parameters.name: queue
parameters.description: Queue name; defaults to the ingest queue.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size for the dead-letter listing (default 50, clamped 1..200).

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Page offset for the dead-letter listing (default 0).

post.description: Move a dead-lettered job back onto its queue for another attempt.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Archive row id of the dead-lettered job

404.description: Job is not in the dead-letter archive

get.description: Operator view of one queue: depth, in-flight, retry histogram, archive tallies.

parameters.name: queue
parameters.description: Queue name; defaults to the ingest queue.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size for the dead-letter listing (default 50, clamped 1..200).

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Page offset for the dead-letter listing (default 0).

get.description: The contradiction queue: oldest first (SLA aging), plus a status histogram that powers the queue's tabs.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: open | resolved_supersede | resolved_coexist | dismissed | all (default open)

parameters.name: min_age_hours
parameters.description: Only rows at least this many hours old

post.description: Resolve an open contradiction by supersede (deprecates the loser), coexist, or dismiss. Supersede requires a maintainer of the losing memory's team.

400.description: Unknown resolution, or supersede without a valid winner_memory_id

403.description: Supersede requires a maintainer of the losing memory's team

404.description: Contradiction not found / not open, or the losing memory is not visible

409.description: Lost a concurrent resolve, or the supersession could not be applied — nothing was resolved

get.summary:
Memories carrying unresolved `wrong` / `outdated` claims from the agents
and operators who were served them — most-disputed first. This is where
MCP memory_feedback verdicts land for a human.
get.description: Memories carrying unresolved wrong/outdated claims from the agents and operators served them, most-disputed first.

parameters.name: min_age_hours
parameters.description: Oldest claim at least this old

parameters.name: min_claims
parameters.description: At least this many open claims

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Filter by project id, or `none` for org-shared

post.summary:
Answer the open claims against a memory. The three answers are the three
things a maintainer can actually mean:
  reverified — checked it, still true → extend its validity window
  deprecated — the reporters are right → end it now, drop it from retrieval
  dismissed  — the reports are noise → leave the memory as-is
Whichever is chosen, every open claim on that memory closes with it.
post.description: Answer every open feedback claim against a memory: reverified (extend validity), deprecated (end it now), or dismissed (memory stands).

403.description: Caller is not a maintainer of the owning team (or, for an org-wide memory, of any team)

404.description: Memory not found (or invisible under RLS)

409.description: Nothing to answer: the claims were already closed by a concurrent maintainer, or the memory's state forbids this resolution

get.description: Promotions awaiting human review (oldest first). Paged: `total` reports the full backlog, `offset` reaches beyond the first page.

post.description: Approve or reject many promotions in one request. Every item is authorized and decided independently — the maintainer gate and the concurrency guard run per item, and the response reports each id's outcome. Returns 200 even when some rows fail; read `results`.

400.description: Unknown action, empty batch, or more than 200 ids

post.description: Approve a pending promotion: moves the memory to the promotion's target status. Requires a maintainer of the owning team.

403.description: Caller is not a maintainer of the owning team

404.description: Promotion not found or no longer pending

post.description: Reject a pending promotion: the memory is marked rejected. Requires a maintainer of the owning team.

403.description: Caller is not a maintainer of the owning team

404.description: Promotion not found or no longer pending

get.summary:
Recent sources with their pipeline rollup — the monitor's feed. Queue
state joins outside RLS (queue schema is org-blind); org membership is
proven by the RLS read of `sources` itself.
get.description: Recent ingest sources with their pipeline rollup (memories, promoted, pending review) and derived queue status.

get.summary:
GET /v1/sources/{id} — what happened to an async memory_add. Closes the
loop on the 202: the source row (RLS-scoped), the queue job state, and
what the pipeline produced from it.
get.description: Ingestion status of a source: queue job state plus what the pipeline produced.

404.description: Source not found (or invisible under RLS — no oracle)

get.summary: GET /v1/tokens — list the org's tokens (metadata only, never secrets).
get.description: List the org's API tokens (metadata only; secrets are never retrievable).

post.description: Mint an API token; the plaintext secret is returned once and never again.

400.description: Empty name or scopes outside the mintable set (see auth::SCOPES)

post.summary:
Blast radius: exactly what a key minted for this principal could read,
computed by opening a transaction AS that principal — the same RLS path
the runtime uses, so the preview can't drift from enforcement.
post.description: Blast-radius preview for a key minted for a given user: what that principal could read, computed under their own RLS. Requires admin scope.

404.description: User not found in this org

post.description: Revoke a token immediately; subsequent requests with it are 401.

404.description: Token not found or already revoked

remote.description: Any spelling of the remote (https, ssh, scp, bare); stored normalized.
The normalized form that was stored — what onboarding will match on.

decree.description:
Adopt WITHOUT evidence, signing for it by name. Absent a decree, a rule
with no provenance is refused — by the database, not this handler.

contradiction_dismiss_rate.description:
Share of RESOLVED contradictions that were dismissed as "not a conflict",
over the last 30 days. Since an unresolved contradiction now WITHHOLDS both
sides from serving (the open-contradiction fix), a high dismiss rate means
an over-eager detector is *suppressing real knowledge*, not just adding
noise — a signal to retune it. `null` if nothing was resolved.

median_time_to_review_secs.description:
Median seconds from a memory entering the queue to a human deciding it,
over the last 30 days. `null` if nothing was reviewed. Against their own
48h SLO (ARCHITECTURE §7) this is the review-latency truth.

reviewed_last_7d.description:
Review VELOCITY — the abandonment signal. A queue with a growing backlog
and near-zero throughput is a review step nobody is working; a healthy one
clears roughly what it takes in. Made observable so the failure the whole
governance model rides on stops being silent (UAT relay
`promotion-queue-backlog`). Human decisions only (auto-approvals excluded).

rubber_stamp_rate.description:
Share of last-30d human reviews decided in under 5s — the rubber-stamp
proxy. High + a deep backlog = clearing, not reviewing. `null` if none.

AnchorRef.description: A canonical entity anchoring a hit (id + name).

ArchivedCounts.description: Archive tallies, kept nested under `archived` (not flattened).

AttemptsBucket.description: One bucket of the retry-attempt histogram over the ready jobs.

AuditEvent.description:
One governance action. `kind` is `promotion_review` |
`contradiction_resolution` | `feedback_resolution`; `memory_b` is only set
for contradictions. `detail` carries the decision's rationale where the path
captures one (the resolution note) and the policy rule otherwise.

actor_id.description: Null for policy (auto) decisions — no human actor.

total.description:
Total governance actions visible to the caller — the full feed length,
independent of the page window.

items.description: Up to `MAX_BULK_ITEMS` (100) items, each the same shape as a single add.

BulkItemResult.description:
One item's outcome, positionally aligned with the request `items`. Either a
success (`source_id` + `job_id`) or a per-item error (`error` + `code`) —
a bad item never sinks the rest of the batch.

action.description: `approve` | `reject` — applied to every id in the batch.

ids.description: The promotions to decide. Duplicates are collapsed.

failed.description:
Rows that were refused or lost a race. The batch still returns 200: the
request succeeded, and `results` says what became of each id.

BulkReviewRow.description:
What happened to ONE promotion in a batch.

`status` is the HTTP status this id would have returned as a single request,
so a partial failure stays legible per row: 403 (not a maintainer of THAT
item's team), 404 (gone, or invisible under RLS), 409 (someone decided it
first). A batch does not collapse these into one error, because they are not
one error — "3 of 12 were not yours" is a different fact from "the request
failed", and only the per-row shape can say it.

error.description: Why this row failed — `None` when it succeeded.

memory_status.description: The memory's status after the decision — `None` when this row failed.

status.description: The status this item would have returned on its own (200/403/404/409/500).

CanonicalEdge.description:
A 1-hop edge touching a surface form; `evidence` is null when the backing
memory is invisible to the caller.

ChainLink.description:
One link of the supersession lineage. `depth` is **signed**: negative for
predecessors (walking back), positive for successors (walking forward).

Citation.description: A memory a page's revision cites, resolved for the reader's provenance popover.

detail_md.description: The artifact behind the claim, when there is one (KB-PLAN D3).

lifecycle.description:
shipped | in_flight | proposed — so the reader can mark a claim that
describes intent rather than production (KB-PLAN D2).

ContradictionMemoryRef.description:
One side of a contradiction. `content` is null when the memory is not
visible to the caller under RLS (the row itself is org-scoped metadata).

counts.description: Status histogram over ALL contradictions — unaffected by the filters.

total.description:
Rows matching the CURRENT filters, ignoring the page window — the real
backlog behind this page. Without it a client can only report the length
of a truncated array, which silently reads as the whole queue the moment
the backlog passes `limit`.

suggested_resolution.description:
`contradictions.resolution_note` — the detector's suggestion, or the
resolver's note once answered.

project_id.description: Scope the key to one project (migration 0034); omit for org-wide.

user_id.description: Principal the token acts as; defaults to the caller.

CreatedTokenResponse.description:
The mint response. `token` is the plaintext secret and is the only place
it will ever appear — everything else is retrievable from `GET /v1/tokens`.

project_id.description: The project the key is scoped to; null = org-wide.

token.description: Shown exactly once — never retrievable again.

DeadLetterEntry.description:
One dead-lettered job. `payload` is the opaque job JSON, passed through
verbatim (its shape belongs to the pipeline, not to this API).

total.description:
Total dead letters on the queue — the full recovery backlog, independent
of the page window, so an operator knows how far `offset` can reach.

oneOf.description: A revision awaiting review, if any.

The published view. `None` for a page whose first revision is still
awaiting a human — nothing publishes itself into existence.

DocFacet.description: One facet option for the wiki (a space, a kind, a status) and its count.

DocFacetMenu.description:
The wiki's facet menu — the space directory and the tab counts — computed
server-side so the client never needs the whole corpus to build its tree.
Present only when `?facets=1`.

dirty.description: Pages behind the corpus under the current filter (the recomposing tab).

needs_review.description: Pages awaiting review under the current filter (the review tab).

spaces.description: The spaces (folders), each with its page count — the tree directory.

faithfulness.description:
Sampled citation-faithfulness verdict (0036), when the runtime judge
ran: `checked` paragraph count plus `flagged` excerpts whose prose
misstates the memory it cites — the reviewer's read-this-first list.
`null` = not judged; absence of a verdict is not a verdict.

DocSectionView.description:
A section as the reader needs to know it. Without this the console cannot
offer an editor at all: an edit needs the section's id, and there is nothing
in the rendered markdown to invent one from. It also carries `mode`, because
the editor must tell a human WHICH KIND of edit they are about to make —
their prose, or a proposal — before they type, not after.

mode.description:
`composed` (a projection — edits become proposed knowledge) or `pinned`
(the human's own prose — edits save).

dirty.description:
An underlying memory changed and the page has not recomposed yet. The
honest signal that what you are reading may already be behind the corpus.

pending_review.description: A revision is waiting on a human. Work, not decoration.

project_id.description:
The project this page is scoped to (PROJECT-PLAN PR4); null = an
org-wide page. A stamped page composes only from its project +
org-shared memories.

oneOf.description: Cross-filtered facet menu, or null when `?facets=1` was not passed.

total.description: Filtered depth — what matches, ignoring the page window.

content.description: The section as the human now wants it to read.

note.description:
Optional: why. Carried into the extraction source, because "why" is
exactly the knowledge a diff cannot recover.

message.description:
What to tell the editor. The wording matters more than it looks — see
the handler.

outcome.description:
`saved` (pinned prose — the human owns it) or `captured` (composed
section — the edit became proposed knowledge).

source_id.description: For a composed edit: the ingest source the edit became.

ErrorResponse.description:
The JSON error body. Documented once in the OpenAPI spec and returned by
every error path.

error.description:
Human-readable message. Specific for business errors (400/401/403/404);
a generic `"internal error"` for 5xx faults (detail is logged, not sent).

ExpiringMemory.description:
A memory in the re-verification horizon. `days_left` is computed per row
against `now()` and is negative once the boundary has passed.

FeedbackClaims.description: Open claim counts against one memory.

FeedbackFacet.description:
One filter value and the disputed-memory count behind it — so a filter
control can show what each option would leave and never offer one that
empties the queue.

FeedbackFacets.description:
The facet breakdown of the FULL backlog, ignoring the current filter — the
menu, which must not shrink as the operator narrows or there is nothing left
to widen back out with.

projects.description: Value is a project id or `"none"`; label the name or `"org-shared"`.

FeedbackProvenance.description:
The provenance record behind the disputed memory. Whole object null (not
omitted) when the memory has none — mirrors `MemoryProvenance` on the
detail endpoint.

facets.description:
The facet menu for building a filter — counts over the FULL backlog, so
it never shrinks as the operator narrows.

total.description:
Memories matching the current filter, ignoring the page window — the
real backlog. `flagged.len()` is only the page; rendering it as the
queue depth understates the moment the backlog passes `limit`.

FeedbackRecordedResponse.description:
The receipt: the verdict as stored (after synonym canonicalization) plus
the memory's running feedback totals.

FeedbackReport.description:
One open claim and the reporter behind it. Deprecating an org memory on an
unattributed tally is guessing; this is what makes it a decision.

age_secs.description:
How long ago this claim was filed. Seconds-since, like every other age
on this payload.

reporter_email.description: Null when the org holds no email for the reporter.

reporter_on_owning_team.description:
The reporter sits on the memory's owning team. Always false for
org-wide memories — there is no owning team to sit on.

FeedbackVerdictCount.description: One verdict tally for a memory.

oldest_claim_secs.description: Age of the OLDEST open claim — how long the dispute has stood.

reporters.description:
DISTINCT reporters behind the open claims — the number that says whether
a tally of five is five people or one agent five times.

reports.description: The open claims themselves (most recent first, capped server-side).

team.description: The owning team's name; null for org-wide memories.

GraphEdge.description:
An evidence edge. `evidence` is null when the backing memory is hidden by
RLS — the edge itself stays visible (it's org metadata).

canonical_id.description: Null when the entity has not been linked to a canonical hub yet.

projects.description:
The project lens (PR3): lobes/links parallel to the team ones. Empty
until writes are project-stamped.

HealthResponse.description: Liveness probe body — `{"status":"ok"}` and nothing else.

HitContradiction.description:
An OPEN contradiction touching a hit (mirrors the MCP search `contradicts`
entries, mcp.rs:557): the contradiction row and the memory it conflicts
with. Reuses [`brainiac_store::governance::open_contradictions_for`].

HitFeedback.description:
What previous readers reported about a hit (mirrors the MCP search
`feedback` block, mcp.rs:544). Present only when the memory carries any
feedback at all — reuses [`brainiac_store::feedback::trust_for`].

disputed.description: True while an unresolved wrong/outdated claim stands — treat as unconfirmed.

severity.description: critical | warning | info — encodes urgency in form, not just number.

consistency.description: 100 − penalty for the org contradicting itself (cross-team conflicts hurt most).

currency.description: Share of the corpus that is still current (not superseded/expired).

governance.description: Is the review queue actually being worked (backlog age vs the 48h SLO).

liquidity.description: How much knowledge crosses team lines — the "together-picture" density.

agent_page_reads_30d.description:
The subset of the last 30 days' reads that came through MCP — coding
agents consuming pages, which is exactly the loop the KB exists for.

cross_project_contradictions.description:
Open contradictions whose two sides are stamped with DIFFERENT
projects — two applications believing incompatible things (PR3).

cross_project_entities.description:
Canonical entities anchored by memories of ≥2 DIFFERENT projects —
knowledge crossing application lines (PROJECT-PLAN PR3). Zero until
writes are project-stamped; that is coverage, not health.

cross_team_contradictions.description:
Open contradictions where the two sides belong to DIFFERENT teams — the ones
no individual team can see. The flagship signal.

cross_team_entities.description: Canonical entities carrying knowledge from ≥2 teams — the graph doing its job.

dirty_page_reads_30d.description:
Reads in the last 30 days that were served while the page was DIRTY —
someone consumed a belief the org had already moved past. This is the
number that ranks rot by harm rather than by age.

liquidity_pct.description: org_wide / total, as a percentage — knowledge liquidity.

oldest_dirty_secs.description:
How long the most overdue page has been out of date, in seconds. THE
propagation SLA: the product's promise is that a resolved contradiction
reaches every page automatically, and this is the number that says
whether "automatically" means minutes or means never.

oldest_gate_secs.description:
How long the oldest candidate has waited, in seconds. Mining and agents
both file into this queue; a queue nobody works makes the whole intake
theatre, and the number says which it is.

page_reads_30d.description:
Page reads served in the last 30 days (0025) — consumption, the half of
liquidity the visibility mix cannot see. Zero on a fresh deployment is
normal; zero six months in means the wiki is decoration.

pages_dirty.description:
Pages whose memories moved and which have NOT recomposed yet. Every one
is a page currently telling readers something the org no longer believes.

pages_never_read.description:
Published pages no one has ever read. Not an emergency — a candidate
list: promote them where readers are, or stop composing them.

pages_pending_review.description: Page revisions awaiting a human — the KB's own review backlog.

skills_dormant.description: Published skills nobody has fetched in a month.

stale_beliefs.description: Superseded/expired beliefs still sitting in the corpus (landmines).

standards_adopted.description: Adopted rules — the org's live, ratified judgment.

standards_at_gate.description: Candidates waiting at the gate (mined, ratified, or agent-proposed).

standards_dormant.description:
Adopted rules the org has had time to use and hasn't touched in a
month. THE Library signal: a standard nobody follows is a wish, and
this is the number that says so without anyone having to notice.

KindProjectCount.description:
The project twin of [`KindTeamCount`] (PROJECT-PLAN PR3). `project` is the
display name, with `org-shared` standing in for the null bucket.

attention.description: Ranked, most-urgent-first — the whole point: turn the score into action.

score.description: 0–100 composite the org tracks week over week.

trend.description:
Recorded snapshots oldest→newest — the score over time. The report's power
is the line, not the point. Empty until the first snapshot is taken.

MemoryAcceptedResponse.description:
The 202 receipt: the source row that was written and the queue job that
will extract memories from it. Poll `GET /v1/sources/{id}` with `source_id`.

entities.description:
Optional entity names this concerns (services/repos/techs/features) —
carried through to resolution so the memory anchors them.

kind.description:
Optional kind hint (fact|decision|pattern|pitfall|howto). For a `manual`
add this is authoritative — the F-3 verbatim path stores the statement
under exactly this kind (default `fact`) with no model guessing.

project_id.description:
PROJECT-PLAN PR0: attribute this write to a project explicitly (must
exist in the caller's org). Omitted ⇒ the key's own project scope, or
org-shared for org-wide keys. An org-wide key (CI, imports) uses this
to attribute correctly.

MemoryFacetMenu.description: The archive's cross-filtered facet menu. Present only when `?facets=1`.

projects.description: Value is a project id or `"none"`; label the name or `"org-shared"`.

oneOf.description: Cross-filtered facet menu, or null when `?facets=1` was not passed.

total.description:
The filtered depth — what matches, ignoring the page window. `memories`
is only the page; this is the number the archive counts by.

MemoryPromotion.description: A promotion attempt on this memory. Timestamps are RFC3339 strings here.

MemoryProvenance.description:
The provenance record behind a memory. The whole object is **null** (not
omitted) when the memory has no provenance row.

MemoryProvenanceResponse.description:
A memory's evidence chain for citation. Mirrors MCP `memory_provenance`
exactly: actor/model/time, the originating source (bounded excerpt), and the
canonical entities it anchors.

MemoryRow.description:
The archive's memory row. NOTE the timestamps here are **RFC3339
strings**, not `DateTime` — this handler stringifies them (unlike the
contradiction/audit/expiring payloads, which pass `DateTime` through).
`created_at` is non-null in practice but is read as an optional column, so
it stays `Option<String>` to keep the emitted shape identical.

project.description: Project display name; null = org-shared (PROJECT-PLAN PR2).

title.description:
A short label for the claim. `None` for anything captured before
migration 0023, and for anything the extractor wrote (it does not
produce one yet) — readers fall back to `content`.

by_project.description: Kind×project volumes — the axis-swap twin of `by_kind` (PR3).

token.description:
The minted key — present exactly once, on the poll that claims an
approved request. Never retrievable again.

project_name.description:
The project this remote would land in (whitelist match in the caller's
org), or null — meaning approval will 409 until the repo is registered.

label.description: Who is asking — hostname/username, shown to the approver.

remote.description: The checkout's `git remote get-url origin`, any spelling.

device_code.description: Long secret the CLI polls with. Appears only here.

remote.description: The normalized remote the request was recorded under.

user_code.description: Short code the approver matches by eye in the console.

verification_url.description: Where a human approves this — the console's projects module.

teams.description:
Raw `json_agg` output — an array of `{id, name, role}` (empty array,
never null, when the user is on no team).

OrgUserTeam.description:
Documentation-only mirror of one element of `OrgUser::teams`. The value is
produced by Postgres `json_agg(json_build_object('id','name','role'))` and
is forwarded verbatim as `serde_json::Value` so the bytes cannot drift;
this struct exists purely to give the schema its real shape.

OverviewCanonical.description:
A canonical hub with its team spread. `teams` is the DISTINCT team count;
`team_ids` is the set itself.

projects.description: DISTINCT projects whose stamped memories anchor this hub (PR3).

PipelineRunRow.description:
One worker run. `started_at` is an RFC3339 **string**; `duration_secs`
measures against `now()` while the run is still open.

total.description:
Total pipeline runs visible to the caller — the full trail length,
independent of the page window.

approaches.description:
Each group's approach: [{team, approach}] on the team axis,
[{project, approach}] on the project axis.

axis.description:
The divergence class (PROJECT-PLAN PR3): `team` (two teams disagree)
or `project` (two applications solve the same thing differently).

model_ref.description: Which model adjudicated it — provenance for a decision-shaping report.

practice.description: The named practice, e.g. "service retry policy".

summary.description: One line: what actually differs between the teams.

divergences.description: Detected divergences, highest-impact first. Empty until the first scan.

ProjectLink.description:
Binding strength between two project lobes = canonicals anchored by
stamped memories of BOTH projects.

ProjectLobe.description:
A project lobe (PROJECT-PLAN PR3): the application/domain plus its
stamped-memory volume and the entities those memories anchor. Only
projects with at least one stamped memory appear — an empty lobe is not
information; org-shared rows belong to every lobe and to none.

PromotionMemory.description:
Memory body of a pending promotion. `None` on the parent ⇒ serialized as
`null` (RLS-invisible memory) — never omitted.

project.description:
Project display name (PROJECT-PLAN PR2); null = org-shared. A reviewer
deciding whether a claim generalizes needs to know which application it
came from.

PromotionProvenance.description: Provenance of a pending promotion; `null` alongside an invisible memory.

total.description:
Total promotions awaiting review — the full backlog, independent of the
page window, so a caller knows how far `offset` can reach.

evidence_memory_id.description:
A memory backing the proposal. Optional — but without evidence the
rule can only ever be adopted by an explicit signed decree.

name.description: Short practice name (the dedup key), e.g. "service retry policy".

outcome.description:
`created` (a fresh candidate waits at the gate) or `duplicate`
(collapsed onto an existing standard — see `lifecycle` for what the
org already decided about this idea).

ProvenanceSource.description:
The originating source, with a bounded excerpt of its raw text. `null` when
the memory carries no source; `excerpt` is `null` when the source has no
text.

excerpt.description:
Bounded to SOURCE_EXCERPT_CHARS (500) chars — a citation handle, never
the whole transcript.

kind.description: `memory` or `divergence` — the evidence class behind the rule.

ProvisionBody.description: A verified federated identity, plus what to do with it.

email.description: For display and support only; never the identity.

issue_key.description:
Mint a fresh device key. Always true in effect on first creation (a project
with no key cannot be reached); on a returning identity it is opt-in, so a
page refresh does not quietly mint keys.

project_name.description: What to call the project. Defaults to the email's local part.

subject.description:
The provider's stable subject — the Firebase uid. NOT the email: emails get
reassigned and change case, uids do not.

api_key.description:
The device key, in the ONLY response that will ever contain it — only its
sha256 is stored. Null when no key was issued this call.

api_key_prefix.description: The key's display prefix, safe to persist and show in a list.

created.description:
False when the identity already had a project. Signing in twice is not an
error — it just does not make a second project.

QueueDepth.description: Ingest queue depth — shared by `/v1/analytics` and the observatory.

standard_id.description:
The standard candidate carrying this divergence as provenance. Stable:
ratifying the same divergence again returns the same candidate.

RequeueResponse.description: Confirmation of a requeue; `requeued` is always `true` (a miss 404s).

winner_memory_id.description: Required for supersede: the memory that wins.

days.description: For `reverified`: the new validity budget (defaults to the kind TTL).

note.description:
Why. Carried into the audit trail as the decision's rationale — the
answer to "who deprecated this org memory, and on what grounds?".
Recorded against the claims being closed, never over the reporter's note.

ResolveFeedbackResponse.description:
`valid_to` is null for `deprecated`/`dismissed` (only `reverified` moves
the boundary) — null, never absent.

days.description: New validity budget from now; defaults to the kind's standard TTL.
The budget actually applied, after clamping.

valid_to.description: The new validity boundary (never null on success).

ReviewDecisionResponse.description:
Outcome of an approve/reject: the promotion, the memory it moved, and the
status that memory now carries.

memory_status.description: The memory's status after the decision.

RevokeResponse.description: Confirmation of a revoke; `revoked` is always `true` (a no-op revoke 404s).

queued.description: The sweep was marked due; the worker picks it up within ~20s.

kinds.description: Memory kinds to keep (fact|decision|pattern|pitfall|howto); omit for all.

project_id.description:
Project lens (PROJECT-PLAN PR1): keeps this project's memories PLUS
org-shared ones (project_id null) — "my project + the org's
conventions". Omit for the unfiltered org view.

anchors.description:
Canonical entities anchoring this hit; for via_graph hits, the bridge it
surfaced through. Empty when the memory has no canonical-linked entities.

contradictions.description: Open contradictions this memory is part of — omitted when there are none.

oneOf.description:
Reader-reported trust signal — present only when this memory carries any
feedback (omitted entirely otherwise, to keep payloads lean).

manifest.description:
The open agent-skill bundle: manifest front-matter, markdown body,
auxiliary resources — exactly as the named human published it.

usage.description: The skill's pulse: fetches/applies per team.

versions.description: Version history, newest first — drafts included, marked as such.

domain.description: Task domain for the catalog, e.g. `database`, `testing`.

instructions_md.description: The whole procedure as markdown — steps, checks, gotchas.

name.description: Short skill name (the dedup key), e.g. "add a data provider".

summary.description: One line: what this skill is for and when to reach for it.

maturity.description: Present on a duplicate: the maturity of the skill this collapsed onto.

outcome.description:
`created` (a fresh DRAFT waits for a maintainer to publish it) or
`duplicate` (collapsed onto an existing skill — see `maturity`).

published.description:
A version nobody signed is a draft; it is listed here for maintainers
but never served as content.

downloadable.description:
Whether a published version exists to download. A draft-only skill is
listed (it exists) but serves no bundle.

total.description:
Total sources visible to the caller — the full feed length, independent
of the page window.

SourceInfo.description: The source row itself, as the caller's RLS scope sees it.

SourceJob.description:
Queue state of the ingest job. `null` on the parent once the job has aged
out of both `queue.jobs` and `queue.archive`.

outcome.description: Archive outcome (`ok` / failure reason); always null while queued.

SourceResults.description: What the pipeline produced from this source (nested, not flattened).

memory_ids.description:
The ids of the memories this source produced — what closes the loop on
an async `memory_add` (F-1/F-2). Poll this endpoint until `status` is
`processed`; then these ids are real memories you can cite (e.g. as a
standard's `evidence_memory_id`) or feed back on. `memory_add` returns a
SOURCE id, not a memory id, and extraction is asynchronous — so before
this, an agent had no way to learn what its contribution became, or
whether it landed at all. Empty while queued, or if extraction produced
nothing (a real outcome worth seeing, not a hang).

SourceRow.description:
A source with its pipeline rollup. `created_at` is an RFC3339 **string**
here (this handler stringifies it). `status` is derived from the queue
job + memory count: queued | retrying | processed | failed | unknown.

attempts.description: Queue delivery attempts; null when no queue job is known.

provenance.description: Why this rule exists. Empty only for a decreed rule.

usage.description: The rule's pulse: fetches/checks per team.

decreed.description: Set only for an evidence-free rule a named human signed for.

detail_md.description: Good/bad examples — served verbatim, never re-typed by a model.

origin.description:
Who created it: `human` (console/ratify), `sweep` (mining), `agent`
(a mid-session proposal). Triage renders this — trust needs a source.

StatusCount.description:
`{status, count}` — the shape every status histogram in this module emits
(memories-by-status, contradiction tabs).

SurfaceForm.description:
A team-scoped surface form linked into the canonical hub, with the link's
confidence and resolution method.

enabled.description: Whether the scheduler runs this sweep on its cadence.

last_detail.description: Human summary of the last run ("7 clusters, 1 divergences") or its error.

last_status.description: 'ok' | 'error' | 'running' — null until it has run once.

next_run_at.description: When the scheduler will next run it (null once disabled and idle).

sweeps.description: Every configured sweep, ordered by kind.

TeamLink.description: Binding strength between two team lobes = canonicals both teams link into.

TeamLobe.description: A team lobe: the team plus its memory/entity volumes.

TeamUsageView.description:
Usage totals for one artifact, per team. There is deliberately no finer
grain to ask for — the storage cannot name a person.

team.description: Team name; `null` groups events from org-scoped (teamless) tokens.

teams.description: Team names, not ids — this is the human-facing picker preview.

TokenSummary.description: One token as the listing exposes it — metadata only, never the secret.

project_id.description: The project the key is scoped to; null = org-wide.

project_name.description: Display name of that project; null = org-wide.

TokenVisibility.description: What a key minted for this principal could read, by visibility tier.

enabled.description: Turn the sweep's schedule on or off.

artifact_id.description:
The artifact's UUID. Optional if `artifact_slug` is given — an agent
holding a slug (what `standards_for` / `skill_fetch` hand back) should
not have to look up the id first, so this mirrors the MCP tool, which
takes a slug. Exactly one of `artifact_id` / `artifact_slug` is required.

artifact_slug.description: The artifact's slug, as an alternative to `artifact_id`.

event.description: `fetch`, `check` (compared work against a standard), or `apply` (ran a skill).

ValidityRow.description:
One row of the as-of skeleton. RFC3339 timestamps, like the rest of the
archive payload.

WeeklyPoint.description:
One point of a weekly series; `week` is an ISO label (`IYYY-Www`) so the
captured/promoted series stay joinable client-side.

WireFacet.description:
One facet option and the count behind it, across the whole console. Value is
what a filter sends back; label is what the UI shows (they differ only for
teams, where the value is the id and the label is the name).

bearer.description: Env-map token (unrestricted) or a managed `brk_…` key carrying the read/write/admin scope the route requires.

tags.name: memories
tags.description: Retrieval, capture, and the memory ledger.

tags.name: reviews
tags.description: Governance queues: promotions, contradictions, disputed memories, and the audit trail.

tags.name: graph
tags.description: The canonical entity graph and its cortex map.

tags.name: analytics
tags.description: Governance health counters and the observatory.

tags.name: ops
tags.description: Operator sweeps: scheduling the periodic org-intelligence scans (admin).

tags.name: queue
tags.description: Job queue health and dead-letter recovery (admin).

tags.name: tokens
tags.description: Managed API keys and their blast radius (admin).

tags.name: projects
tags.description: The project registry + repo whitelist keys scope to (admin).

tags.name: onboard
tags.description: Developer onboarding: device-flow pairing that mints project-scoped keys, driven by the brainiac-onboard skill.
